Inglis Premier Yearling Sale: Five things we learned on Day 1

A Zoustar filly offered by Two Bays Farm turned the most heads on the first day of the 2026 Inglis Premier Yearling Sale, but there was also a flurry of attention for offspring of Ghaiyyath, Toronado, and Extreme Choice.

It is two months into the yearling sales season, and we take a moment to pause and reflect on how the season has started for some of the current crop of first season sires. Who has been meeting expectations, and who is patiently waiting for results beyond the ring?

Meydan's Super Saturday card went ahead despite military action in the Middle East which saw a fire break out at Dubai's luxury Fairmont The Palm Hotel, 25km away from Meydan Racecourse. Godolphin’s Rebel's Romance celebrated his 21st career victory, while Too Darn Hot sired a stakes double.
In today's 'The TTR Ten' - Inglis Premier Yearling Sales Edition - David Feek from Feek Racing answers 10 rapid-fire questions.

On a spectacular day of top-tier racing in Sydney and Melbourne, it was Autumn Glow who shone brightest, stretching her flawless record to a perfect 10 out of 10 in the G1 Verry Elleegant Stakes at Randwick. Meanwhile, Godolphin and trainer Ciaron Maher also celebrated a remarkable interstate Group 1 double, with Observer claiming the Australian Guineas at Flemington following Tempted’s breakthrough victory in the Surround Stakes.

It was a day where three 2-year-olds across Australia cemented themselves into guaranteed Golden Slipper starts and another is on the brink, trainers and sires landed quinellas, and a pair of commanding performances at Eagle Farm neatly rounded out a high-quality afternoon.

On Saturday, the racing world mourned the loss of one of its greatest champions, Makybe Diva, with news that the three-time G1 Melbourne Cup heroine had passed away at 27, leaving a legacy that captivated racing fans around the world.
In today's 'The TTR Ten' - Melbourne Premier Yearling Sales Edition - Anthony Manton from Patriot Bloodstock answers 10 rapid-fire questions.
